title: Name/secure the biostatistician + licensing/provenance reviewers (blocking gate roles)
context: The stats-for-clinicians project requires a credentialed biostatistician/epidemiologist sign-off on every explainer before it ships — this is a zero-tolerance, non-skippable accuracy gate. Until at least one named, qualified reviewer is in place, authoring halts and no explainer can be published. A second named reviewer is strongly preferred to enable rotation and cross-checking. Similarly, a licensing & provenance reviewer must be named to verify that every example dataset has a verified open license and every conceptual claim is cited. These roles are the single most critical dependency for the entire project.
objective: Identify and confirm at least one credentialed biostatistician or epidemiologist as the statistical-accuracy reviewer and at least one licensing/provenance reviewer before the M0 pilot is reviewed, and document their names, credentials, and agreement to serve in a committed role file.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one named, credentialed biostatistician or epidemiologist has agreed to serve as the statistical-accuracy reviewer; their name, credentials (degree/affiliation/relevant experience), and confirmation of role are recorded in a committed document.

At least one named licensing & provenance reviewer has agreed to verify dataset licenses, claim citations, reproducibility, and no copyrighted-figure reuse; their name and confirmation of role are recorded.

The reviewer roster document is committed to the project repo (e.g. REVIEWERS.md or equivalent); it specifies the non-skippable nature of the stats sign-off gate and that a zero-falsehood rule applies.
If a rotation of >= 2 qualified statistical reviewers is secured, both are named; the document notes that >= 1 named reviewer must always exist or authoring halts.
The document is consistent with the authoring template's review block fields (statsReviewer, statsSignoff, clinicalReviewer, plainLanguageReviewer).
The steward/maintainer has acknowledged the reviewer roster and the authoring-halt condition in writing (e.g. a PR approval comment or a recorded meeting note).
resources: TASKS.md — M0 blocking-gate description and reviewer-001 row
Elyos good-deed definition: riskTier:medium requires credentialed reviewer sign-off
output: A committed reviewer-roster document (e.g. REVIEWERS.md) naming the statistical-accuracy reviewer(s) and the licensing/provenance reviewer(s), recording their credentials and agreement, and stating the non-skippable sign-off condition. The document unblocks all M0 explainer authoring and review tasks.
